report:
## Your Health Assessment

Hi Justin, thanks for taking the time to complete this assessment. Your answers paint a clear and cohesive picture, and the good news is that the patterns we're seeing are well understood and very addressable. Let's walk through what stood out.

### Your Allergies Are a Gut-Immune Problem

This is the big one. You've been managing your allergies with antihistamines, immunotherapy, and avoidance strategies for what sounds like a long time, and yet things are getting worse year over year. That progression is the key detail. If this were simply a matter of your immune system being "oversensitive," it wouldn't be steadily escalating. Something is actively driving the dysfunction deeper.

Roughly 70% of your immune system lives in and around your gut. When the gut barrier becomes compromised, your immune system loses its ability to distinguish between genuine threats and harmless substances like pet dander, pollen, or food proteins. The result is exactly what you're experiencing: reactions that broaden over time, congestion that never fully resolves, and histamine responses that seem to fire at lower and lower thresholds. The fact that your allergies are year-round with no seasonal pattern, worst in the morning, and progressively worsening all point toward a systemic issue rooted in the gut rather than a simple environmental trigger problem.

### The Histamine Cycle Is Active

Your reactions to wine and aged foods confirm that histamine is a central player here. This is important because it tells us something about the mechanism. Gut dysbiosis promotes the activation of immune cells called mast cells, which release histamine. That histamine drives more inflammation, which further damages the gut barrier, which worsens dysbiosis, which activates more mast cells. It becomes a self-reinforcing loop, and it explains why things keep escalating despite your best efforts to manage them from the outside.

Your white tongue coating in the morning is a well-recognized indicator of bacterial imbalance in the gut. Combined with bloating, it suggests the microbial environment in your digestive tract is off, and that imbalance is likely feeding directly into the histamine cycle that's driving your congestion, flushing, and headaches.

### Your Metabolism May Be Quietly Contributing

A few things caught our attention here. Your energy is moderate at best, and you're frequently cold in your extremities. Cold fingers, toes, and nose are one of the more reliable signs that thyroid function and overall metabolic output may not be where they should be. This matters for your allergies more than you might think.

Thyroid hormones drive gut motility, the movement of food through the digestive tract. When motility slows, bacteria have more time to ferment food, produce gas (your bloating), and proliferate in places they shouldn't be. Sluggish metabolism also impairs the immune system's ability to regulate itself and weakens the gut barrier's capacity to repair. So what looks like a separate issue (low energy, cold hands) may actually be part of the same web that's driving your immune dysfunction.

### Your Eating Pattern Deserves a Closer Look

You're fasting through the morning on black coffee and not eating until lunch. While this is a common pattern, it can work against you in a few specific ways. Skipping breakfast and relying on caffeine alone can elevate stress hormones like cortisol and adrenaline to mobilize energy. Over time, this kind of low-grade metabolic stress can suppress thyroid output, impair gut motility, and shift your immune system toward the exact state that amplifies allergic responses. Your body may have adapted to this pattern so it feels normal, but it could be quietly contributing to the picture we're seeing.

---

A quiz like this can identify patterns, but it can only go so far. Your situation has layers, and a real conversation would let us dig into the specifics of your history, your timeline, and what's been tried, so we can understand exactly what's driving this for you.

We offer a completely free consultation with someone on our team who understands this approach inside and out. No cost, no obligation, just an honest look at what might be going on and whether we can help. If you'd like to explore that, you can book using the button below.
